subject: Chemistry
q:
In the following molecules,

Hybridisation of carbon a,b and c respectively are :
question:
In the following molecules,

Hybridisation of carbon a,b and c respectively are :
solution:
Hybridisation is found by steric number which is generally the summation of sigma bonds and lone pairs of electrons.

So as we can see a has four sigma bonds so it has sp3 hybridisation.

b,c has three sigma bonds so it has sp2 hybridisation.

q:
A chloro compound "A".
(i) forms aldehydes on ozonolysis followed by the hydrolysis.
(ii) when vaporized completely 1.53 g of A, gives 448 mL of vapour at STP. The number of carbon atoms in a molecule of compound A is
question:
A chloro compound "A".
(i) forms aldehydes on ozonolysis followed by the hydrolysis.
(ii) when vaporized completely 1.53 g of A, gives 448 mL of vapour at STP. The number of carbon atoms in a molecule of compound A is
solution:
448 mL of A gives 1.53 g of A

22400 mL gives 1.53448×22400 g of A=76.5 g

CH3-CH=CH-Cl→O3, Zn/H2OCH3CHO (aldehyde)

CH3-CH=CH-Cl has 3 -carbon atoms and molecular mass =76.5.

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of the following statement is correct for paper chromatography?
question: Which of the following statement is correct for paper chromatography?
options: Water present in the pores of the paper forms the stationary phase.
Paper sheet forms the stationary phase.
Water present in the mobile phase gets absorbed by the paper which then forms the stationary phase.
Paper and water present in its pores together form the stationary phase.
solution: The principle of paper chromatography is partition. In paper chromatography there are two phases one is the stationary phase and the other is the mobile phase. Here, water trapped in the paper acts as the stationary phase and solvent acts as the mobile phase.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Match List-I with List-II.

  List-I   List-II
A

I Spiro compound
B

II Aromatic compound
C

III Non-planar Heterocyclic compound
D

IV Bicyclo compound

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
question:
Match List-I with List-II.

  List-I   List-II
A

I Spiro compound
B

II Aromatic compound
C

III Non-planar Heterocyclic compound
D

IV Bicyclo compound

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
solution:
A spiro compound, or spirane, is a chemical compound, typically an organic compound, that presents a twisted structure of two or more rings (a ring system), in which 2 or 3 rings are linked together by one common atom.

Bicyclo compounds are a class of saturated compounds consisting of two fused rings, having two or more atoms in common, and that take the name of an open chain hydrocarbon containing the same total number of atoms.

Aromatic compounds are chemical compounds that consist of conjugated planar ring systems accompanied by delocalised pi-electron clouds in place of individual alternating double and single bonds.

Heterocyclic compounds are cyclic compounds with the ring containing carbon and other element, the component being oxygen, nitrogen and sulphur.

The name of is tetrahydro furan. The name of  is furan. The name of  is 2,2,4,4-tetramethylbicyclo[1.1.0]butane. The name of  is 1,2,4,5-tetramethylspiro[2.2]pentane

subject: Chemistry
q: Lassaigne's test is used for detection of:
question: Lassaigne's test is used for detection of:
solution:
Lassaigne's test helps to detect Nitrogen, Sulphur, and Halogens in organic compounds. Here, sodium metal Na is heated in the presence of the organic compound inside a fusion tube. The primary objective of this test is that all the available elements are converted by Sodium metal Na into the ionic state. Lassaigne test reaction for sodium is given below:

N+Na+C→NaCN

S+2Na→Na2S

X+Na→NaX

In the third experiment, X can be anything such as Cl, Br or I.

Compounds having same number of carbon atoms follow the boiling point order as:
$(\text { Boiling point })_{\text {Hydrogen bonding }}>(\text { Boiling point })_{\text {high polanity }}>$ $(\text { Boiling point })_{\text {low polarity }}>(\text { Boiling point })_{\text {non polar }}$

q: On the treatment of the following compound with a strong acid, the most susceptible site for bond cleavage is:
question: On the treatment of the following compound with a strong acid, the most susceptible site for bond cleavage is:
solution: The lone pair of electrons on $\mathrm{O} 2$ is involved in resonance with $\mathrm{C}=\mathrm{C}$. Hence $\mathrm{O} 2$ will not be protonated. The lone pair of electrons on O5 is not involved in resonance with $\mathrm{C}=\mathrm{C}$. Hence, O5 will be protonated. Chloride ion will then attack least substituted $\mathrm{C}$ atom (C6)

subject: Chemistry
q:
Resonance in carbonate ion CO32- is

Which of the following is true?
question:
Resonance in carbonate ion CO32- is

Which of the following is true?
options: It is possible to identify each structure individually by some physical or chemical method.
All these structures are in dynamic equilibrium with each other.
Each structure exists for equal amount of time.

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msubsup><mi>CO</mi><mn>3</mn><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msubsup></math> has a single structure i.e., resonance hybrid of the above three structures.
solution:
Resonating structure are hypothetical and resonance hybrid is real structure which is weighted average of all the resonating structures.

So, CO32- has a single structure i.e. resonance hybrid of the three structures.

subject: Chemistry
q: 1.4 g of an organic compound was digested according to Kjeldahl's method and the ammonia evolved was absorbed in 60 mL of M/10 <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msub></math> solution. The excess sulphuric acid required 20 mL of M/10 NaOH solution for neutralization. The percentage of nitrogen in the compound is:
question: 1.4 g of an organic compound was digested according to Kjeldahl's method and the ammonia evolved was absorbed in 60 mL of M/10 <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msub></math> solution. The excess sulphuric acid required 20 mL of M/10 NaOH solution for neutralization. The percentage of nitrogen in the compound is:
solution:
Total millimoles of sulphuric acid =60×110=6 mm H2SO4

Excess H2SO4≡20×110×12=1 mm H2SO4

H2SO4 used in reaction with ammonia =6-1=5 mm

Based on the reaction stoichiometry, 2NH3+H2SO4⟶NH42SO4

mm of NH3=10 mm

Mass of N=10×10-3×14gmole=0.140g

% N2=0.1401.4×100=10%

q: The increasing order of the pKa values of the following compounds is:
question: The increasing order of the pKa values of the following compounds is:
solution:
pKa=-logKa

Ka↑pKa↓ & vice versa

Acidic character α Ka α1pKa

Acidic character α stability of anion (Conjugate base)

Conjugate base are


Stability of conjugate base α-M or -I 
α1+M or1+I
-M is more dominating than -I

( B )    −NO2 at para so exerts -M or -I  &  -M > -I

So it is highly stable anion in

( C )  -NO2 at meta exerts only  -I effect

( A )  No e- withdrawing or electron releasing group.

( D )  -OCH3 has +M & -I but +M dominates

So acidic strength will be

B>C>A>D

Therefore pKa values will be in the order

B<C<A<D

question:

solution:
Molecular mass of Barium Sulphate(BaSO4) = 233 g
%sulphur=32233× weight of BaSO4 formed  weight of organic compound ×100

=32233×1.44390.471×100

=42.10

Nearest integer: 42

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of the following compound is added to the sodium extract before addition of silver nitrate for testing of halogens?
question: Which of the following compound is added to the sodium extract before addition of silver nitrate for testing of halogens?
solution:
For testing of halogens, Nitric acid is added to the sodium extract because if CN- or S2- are present then they will be oxidised and removed before the test of halides.

NaCN+HNO3→NaNO3+HCN↑
Na2S+HNO3→NaNO3+H2S↑
Nilnic acid decomposed NaCN & Na2S, else they precipitate in test & misquite the resolve

subject: Chemistry
q: In Carius method, 0.75 g of an organic compound gave 1.2 g of barium sulphate, find percentage of sulphur (molar mass $32 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$). Molar mass of barium sulphate is $233 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$.
question: In Carius method, 0.75 g of an organic compound gave 1.2 g of barium sulphate, find percentage of sulphur (molar mass $32 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$). Molar mass of barium sulphate is $233 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$.
solution:
In Carius method, sulfur is converted to $BaSO_4$. Each mole of $BaSO_4$ contains 1 mole of S.
Mass of S = $\frac{32}{233} \times 1.2 = 0.1648$ g
Percentage of S = $\frac{0.1648}{0.75} \times 100 = 21.97\%$

q: Following Kjeldahl's method, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> of organic compound released ammonia, that neutralised <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><msub><mi>MH</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>SO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mo>.</mo></math> The percentage of nitrogen in the compound is _______ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>%</mo><mo>.</mo></math>
question: Following Kjeldahl's method, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> of organic compound released ammonia, that neutralised <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><msub><mi>MH</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>SO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mo>.</mo></math> The percentage of nitrogen in the compound is _______ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>%</mo><mo>.</mo></math>
solution:
H2SO4+2NH3→NH42SO4

Equivalent of NH3=Equivalent of H2SO4

moles of NH3nNH3×1=2×10×21000

                                            =4×10-2 moles

Moles of nitrogen=4×10-2 moles

Weight of Nitrogen=4×10-2×14=56×10-2 gm

Percent of nitrogen=56×10-2weight of the compound×100=56×10-21×100=56%

q: In Carius method, halogen containing organic compound is heated with fuming nitric acid in the presence of:
question: In Carius method, halogen containing organic compound is heated with fuming nitric acid in the presence of:
solution:
Organic compound is heated with fuming nitric acid in the presence of silver nitrate in carius method. Lunar caustic AgNO3 is used as reagent hare to distinguish Cl-,Br and I- respectively as follows.

Cl-aq⟶AgNO3AgCl↓ppt white

Br-aq⟶AgNO3AgBr↓ppt pale yellow

I-aq⟶AgNO3AgI↓ppt Dark yellow

q: Which of the following molecules is expected to rotate the plane of plane polarized light?
question: Which of the following molecules is expected to rotate the plane of plane polarized light?
solution:
The plane of polarized light is rotated by optically active compound, i.e. it should be chiral. 
So, (A) has, chiral $\mathrm{C-}$atom. So, it is optically active.
In (B), (C) and (D) plane of symmetry is present.
Hence, (A) is correct.

q: The strongest acid amongst the following compounds is :

solution:
Electron releasing groups (Alkyl groups) de stabilizes conjugate base.
The $+$ I effect of $\mathrm{C}_3 \mathrm{H}_7$ is less than $-1$ effect of $\mathrm{Cl}$
$\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{a}}$ of $\mathrm{HCOOH}$ is $17.9 \times 10^{-5}$
